# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

# Update package list
RUN apt-get update

# Install required system utilities
RUN apt-get install -y curl git

# Install Node.js
RUN apt-get install -y nodejs

# Install build tools
RUN apt-get install -y build-essential

# Install other dependencies (e.g. npm, yarn)
RUN apt-get install -y npm

# Set working directory to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package.json into the container
COPY package*.json ./

# Install dependencies
RUN npm install

# Copy application code
COPY . .

# Expose port 3000 (assuming Node.js app listens on this port)
EXPOSE 3000

# Run the command to start the development server
CMD ["npm", "start"]
